sections.paragraph:
μάχη, ης, ἡ (s. μάχομαι; Hom.+) ‘battle’ (one fighter on each side is enough: Maximus Tyr. 22, 4b), in our lit. only in pl. and only of battles fought without actual weapons fighting, quarrels, strife, disputes (Pythag., Ep. 5, 7; SIG 1109, 72; Kaibel 522, 5; PRyl 28, 203 μάχας ἕξει διὰ θῆλυ; Cat. Cod. Astr. XII 160, 1 of marital discord; LXX, Philo; Ar. 13, 5 μ. καὶ διαφωνία) w. πόλεμοι (Il. 5, 891 πόλεμοί τε μάχαι τε; Dio Chrys. 21 [38], 11; Plut., Mor. 108a) Js 4:1. ἔξωθεν μάχαι ἔσωθεν φόβοι 2 Cor 7:5. γεννᾶν μάχας breed quarrels 2 Ti 2:23. μάχαι νομικαί strife about the law Tit 3:9 (cp. Pla., Tim. p. 88a μάχας ἐν λόγοις ποιεῖσθαι).—DELG s.v. μάχομαι. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. pl. = plural

Pythag Pythag = letters attributed to the phil. Pythagoras (VI–V b.c.); s. Ep.; date uncertain—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 622
